nodes.en: Maps and atlases are spatial representations showing geography, political boundaries, infrastructure networks, or thematic data distribution. They encode complex geographic relationships into visual form.
nodes.cs: Mapy a atlasy jsou prostorové reprezentace ukazující zeměpis, politické hranice, infrastrukturní sítě nebo distribuci tematických dat. Kódují složité geografické vztahy do vizuální podoby.
nodes.brief:
Maps and atlases are spatial representations showing geography, political boundaries, infrastructure networks, or thematic data distribution. They encode complex geographic relationships into visual form, letting a reader grasp scale, distance, and adjacency at a glance that prose description cannot convey as efficiently.

Within [[09]] Reference & Documentation, this branch is the reference counterpart to the applied skill covered in [[08.06]] Navigation: a map is the artifact, navigation is the practice of reading and correlating it against real terrain. It also relates to [[09.10]] Historical Archives, since historical maps are themselves primary sources documenting how borders, settlements, and place names changed over time, and to [[01]] Natural Sciences, since thematic maps of climate, geology, or ecosystems apply earth-science data to cartographic form.

For a comprehensive open cartographic dataset, see [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/), and for authoritative historical map collections, the [David Rumsey Map Collection](https://www.davidrumsey.com/).
